reasoning: Based on the FRED data, the NFCI Credit Subindex has exhibited strong downward momentum over the last two months, decreasing steadily from a high of +0.022 on April 18, 2025 to -0.036 on June 20, 2025. Given 9 consecutive weeks of decline, the short-term probability of an increase by June 29 is low, as the persistent trend heavily favors further downside or stagnation. However, the velocity of the decline has been slowing (narrowing from a drop of 0.009 per week to 0.004 per week recently), suggesting a potential bottoming in the medium term. The subindex is constructed to be mean-reverting over the long term (with an average of zero), so from its current mildly negative level of -0.036, there is an eventual pull upward or flatting out. To comply with the guidance that forecasting uncertainty should increase with horizon, the probabilities converge to 0.5 as the time horizon extends to six months.
